entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., a rainfed crop grown largely by resource-poor farmers in semi-arid tropics

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The output level of groundnut, which is limited by biotic and abiotic stresses

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of enhancing groundnut productivity to ensure food availability

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where groundnut is grown and productivity is targeted

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Environmental factors including diseases and insect pests that limit groundnut productivity

entities.name: ARACHIS SPECIES
entities.description: Wild relatives of groundnut that harbor genes for yield, quality, and resistance

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: A productivity attribute that can be improved using genes from wild Arachis species

entities.name: SEED QUALITY
entities.description: A quality attribute that can be improved using genes from wild Arachis species

entities.name: DISEASE AND PEST RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability to withstand biotic stresses, found in wild Arachis species

entities.name: WIDE CROSSES
entities.description: Breeding between cultivated groundnut and wild Arachis species to liberate resistance genes

entities.name: LINKAGE DRAG
entities.description: The inheritance of undesirable traits along with beneficial genes during wide crosses

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BACKCROSS BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding method used to minimize linkage drag and monitor introgressed chromosome segments

entities.name: GENETIC TRANSFORMATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used to produce transgenic groundnuts with resistance or tolerance to stresses

entities.name: MARKER-ACCELERATED INTROGRESSION
entities.description: A method to introduce favorable genes into cultivated groundnut using markers

entities.name: DNA MARKER BASED GENETIC LINKAGE MAP
entities.description: A tool used to enable breeders to effectively pyramid genes for quality and resistance

entities.name: GENE PYRAMIDING
entities.description: The process of combining multiple beneficial genes into breeding populations

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Traditional breeding methods that are slower than molecular breeding techniques

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat markers used to detect genetic variation in groundnut landraces

entities.name: PCR-BASED MARKERS
entities.description: Markers developed via PCR, including SSRs and SNPs, used for genetic linkage maps

entities.name: SNPS
entities.description: Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms used for the construction of high-density genetic linkage maps

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING
entities.description: The application of molecular tools to improve groundnut productivity and resistance

entities.name: AUTOMATED TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: High-throughput tools used for large-scale germplasm characterization and marker-assisted selection

entities.name: GERMPLASM CHARACTERIZATION
entities.description: The process of analyzing the genetic makeup of plant collections

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: The use of genetic markers to select for desired traits in groundnut

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL LEGUME GENOMICS INITIATIVE
entities.description: A collaborative effort to translate the benefits of the consensus legume genome for tropical legumes

entities.name: USA
entities.description: Location of universities participating in the international legume genomics initiative

entities.name: ECO-REGIONAL ALLIANCE ON LEGUMES
entities.description: International Agricultural Research Centers collaborating on legume genomics

entities.name: TROPICAL LEGUMES
entities.description: Legumes grown in tropical regions targeted for genetic improvement

entities.name: CONSENSUS LEGUME GENOME
entities.description: A reference genome used to provide rapid impacts on the genetic improvement of tropical legumes
